hawkish_phrases: A majority of participants emphasized upside risks to their outlooks for inflation
progress toward the Committee's 2 percent inflation objective had stalled
possibility that elevated inflation proves to be more persistent than currently expected
if policy were eased too much or too soon and inflation continued to be elevated, then longer-term inflation expectations could become unanchored
A few participants stated there was merit in keeping the federal funds rate unchanged at this meeting
dovish_phrases: downside risks to employment had increased over the intermeeting period
job gains had slowed and the unemployment rate had edged up
if policy rates were kept too high for too long, then unemployment could rise unnecessarily
Miran preferred to lower the target range for the federal funds rate by 1/2 percentage point
summary: The Committee voted 11-1 to cut the federal funds rate by 25 basis points to 4.00-4.25%, with one dissent preferring a 50bp cut, citing increased downside risks to employment while acknowledging that inflation remains somewhat elevated and most participants emphasize upside inflation risks remain.
